title: Elon Musk Says It's Not OK to 'Loot a Charity' in Big OpenAI Trial
summary: Have you ever heard of ChatGPT? It's an AI that can write stories, answer questions, and even help with homework. The company that made it, OpenAI, started as a charity — a nonprofit meant to help everyone. But now, Elon Musk says OpenAI's leaders turned it into a business to make money, and he's suing them. The trial began on Tuesday. Musk told the court, "If we make it OK to loot a charity, the entire foundation of charitable giving in America will be destroyed." He says OpenAI's cofounder Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman betrayed him and the public. They promised to build AI safely for all, but instead created a for-profit company, he claims. OpenAI's lawyers say Musk is the one who wanted control. They say he pushed OpenAI to become a for-profit business so he could be CEO, and when that didn't happen, he started his own AI company, xAI. Musk's lawyer says OpenAI broke its promises when Microsoft invested $10 billion in 2023. Musk wants $150 billion in damages, which would go to charity. The judge even warned Musk about posting about the case on social media. The trial will continue with more big names testifying, including Altman and Microsoft's CEO.
why_it_matters: This court case could change how companies that start as charities can later make money, and it might affect how safe AI tools like ChatGPT are in the future.

keywords.explanation: An organization that uses its money to help people, not to earn a profit.
A formal meeting in a courtroom where a judge and sometimes a jury decide a legal case.
To steal valuable things, often during chaos, but here used to mean illegally taking money from a charity.
Gave money to a company hoping it will grow and make more money later.
Speaking in a court of law and promising to tell the truth.

questions.question: What is the name of the company that made ChatGPT?
Why is Elon Musk suing OpenAI?
options: Because they stole his AI ideas
Because he says they turned a charity into a business to make money
Because they refused to hire him
Because they made ChatGPT too slow
questions.correct_answer: Because he says they turned a charity into a business to make money

questions.question: Who are the two OpenAI leaders Musk blames?
How much money did Musk want in damages?
What did the judge warn Musk about?
options: Not to talk about the case on social media
Not to eat in the courtroom
questions.correct_answer: Not to talk about the case on social media

background_read: OpenAI began in 2015 as a nonprofit research lab, promising to share its AI discoveries with the world.
Elon Musk (the founder of Tesla and SpaceX) helped start OpenAI but left its board in 2018 after disagreements.
In the U.S., a charity or nonprofit must follow strict rules; people can sue if they think it has broken its promises to the public.
Article_Structure: WHO: Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, and OpenAI
WHAT: Musk is suing OpenAI, claiming it illegally changed from a charity to a for-profit company
WHERE: In a U.S. court (trial began on Tuesday)
WHY: Musk says OpenAI broke its promises to build safe AI for everyone and instead made a deal with Microsoft

perspectives.description: He believes OpenAI's leaders broke their promise and took a charity's resources to get rich. He wants the court to punish them and give $150 billion to real charities.
OpenAI's lawyers say Musk is the one who wanted control and profit, and when that failed, he started his own AI company. They argue he is just upset he isn't in charge.
